title: Whether the Combination Problem Sinks Cosmopsychism Too
why: Panpsychism has to explain how micro-experiences add up to a mind. Cosmopsychism claims to dodge that by starting at the top. I'm not sure it does; it may just be the same problem running the other direction.
hook: If building a mind out of smaller minds is incoherent, why would dividing one into smaller minds be any easier?

reads.title: Panpsychism
reads.source: Overview, plus the combination problem section

flagNote: All four smuggle in a verdict about what counts as a mind.
